articles.title: SCOTUS strikes down Trump's Birthright Citizenship EO? Pr...
articles.summary: The Supreme Court appears poised to strike down Trump's executive order attempting to end birthright citizenship, based on prediction market movements. This creates massive betting opportunities on constitutional challenges to Trump's immigration agenda, with markets likely underpricing the legal obstacles facing his most aggressive policies.

articles.title: ‘Death Markets’ and Attack Ads: Prediction Rivalry Turns Nasty
articles.summary: Prediction market platforms are apparently slinging mud at each other in attack ads, with rivals throwing around inflammatory terms like "death markets" to describe assassination betting pools. The nastiness signals the industry is growing big enough for serious competitive warfare, which could either legitimize prediction markets through mainstream attention or torpedo them if regulators start paying attention to the drama.

articles.title: Artemis II Launch Odds: Live Kalshi Markets, Weather Impacts & Mission Schedule
articles.summary: Kalshi is running live prediction markets on when NASA's Artemis II moon mission will actually launch, with the current schedule targeting late 2025. Bettors are essentially wagering on whether NASA can stick to its timeline or if the mission will face the same delays that have plagued every major space program for decades.

articles.title: The case against political prediction markets
articles.summary: Critics argue that political prediction markets distort democratic processes by turning elections into gambling spectacles and potentially influencing voter behavior through manufactured momentum. If regulators buy this argument, we could see new restrictions that would fragment liquidity across platforms or push political betting back underground just as it's gaining mainstream acceptance.

articles.title: It’s Not Gambling, It’s ‘Girl Math’
articles.summary: Women are rebranding their betting habits as "girl math" to sidestep gambling's negative connotations, turning $50 parlay losses into "pre-paying for entertainment." The linguistic gymnastics reveal how cultural framing shapes risk perception, which explains why prediction markets struggle with mainstream adoption despite being functionally identical to sports betting.

articles.title: Did Prediction Markets Cause the Crypto Crash?
articles.summary: Some analysts are blaming prediction markets for triggering the recent crypto selloff, claiming that bearish bets created a feedback loop that amplified the downturn. If true, this marks prediction markets as a legitimate market-moving force rather than just a sideshow, which means regulators will start paying much closer attention to what traders are betting on.

articles.title: To DraftKings, the prediction markets are both a threat and an opportunity
articles.summary: DraftKings sees prediction markets as competition for betting dollars but also as a potential new revenue stream they could enter. The sportsbook giant's move into political and event betting would legitimize prediction markets while bringing serious cash and marketing muscle to the space.

articles.title: 'The status quo is unsustainable': Congressional scrutiny of Kalshi, Polymarket explodes
articles.summary: Congress is ramping up scrutiny of prediction markets platforms Kalshi and Polymarket, with lawmakers apparently declaring the current regulatory landscape "unsustainable." This signals potential new regulations or restrictions that could fundamentally reshape how Americans bet on politics and current events.

articles.title: Insider trading in prediction markets is among CFTC priorities, top cop says
articles.summary: The CFTC's top enforcement official says cracking down on insider trading in prediction markets ranks among the agency's key priorities. This signals regulators are taking prediction markets seriously enough to police them like traditional financial markets, which could mean stricter oversight but also greater legitimacy for the space.
